introduction.title: What is Self Introduction?
introduction.explanation: Self Introduction means presenting yourself to one or more people for the first time. It is the most important skill in English communication because it creates your first impression. A good self-introduction tells people: (1) who you are, (2) where you are from, (3) what you do, (4) what you are interested in, and (5) what your goals are.
introduction.hindiExplanation: Self Introduction का मतलब है पहली बार किसी को या कुछ लोगों को अपना परिचय देना। यह English communication में सबसे जरूरी skill है क्योंकि यही आपकी first impression बनाती है। एक अच्छा self-introduction बताता है: (1) आप कौन हैं, (2) आप कहाँ से हैं, (3) आप क्या करते हैं, (4) आपकी क्या रुचि है, और (5) आपके क्या goals हैं।
importance: Essential for job interviews and networking
Establishes your identity in professional settings
whenToUse: When meeting someone for the first time
On the first day of a new job

basicStructure.title: The 5-Part Structure of a Perfect Self Introduction

parts.name: Opening / Greeting
parts.explanation: Always start with a warm, confident greeting. This sets the tone for your introduction.
examples: Good morning! / Good afternoon! / Good evening!
parts.tip: Smile while greeting — it makes you look confident and friendly.

parts.name: Name and Origin
parts.explanation: State your full name and where you are from. This is the most basic information.
examples: I belong to Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.
formulas: I was born in + [Place] but raised in + [Place]
parts.tip: In formal settings, use your full name. In informal settings, just your first name is fine.

parts.name: Education or Profession
parts.explanation: Tell about your studies or job. This establishes your professional identity.
examples: I am pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science from Delhi University.
I work as a software engineer at TCS.
I am a final year student of MBA.
I have 3 years of experience in digital marketing.
formulas: I work as a/an + [Job Title] at + [Company]
I have + [Number] years of experience in + [Field]
I am currently working in + [Industry / Field]
parts.tip: Mention the most relevant qualification or experience for your audience.

parts.name: Hobbies and Interests
parts.explanation: Share 2-3 of your hobbies. This shows your personality and makes you memorable.
examples: In my free time, I love reading books and traveling.
I am passionate about photography and music.
Outside of work, I enjoy playing cricket and cooking.
formulas: In my free time, I love + [hobby]
When I am not working, I like to + [activity]
parts.tip: Choose hobbies that relate to the skills required in your role or show positive traits.

parts.name: Goals and Closing
parts.explanation: End with your goals or a positive closing statement. This shows ambition and leaves a strong final impression.
examples: My goal is to become a full-stack developer within two years.
I am looking forward to contributing to this organization.
I am excited about this opportunity to learn and grow.
Thank you for giving me this opportunity to introduce myself.
formulas: Thank you for the opportunity to introduce myself.
parts.tip: Keep the closing positive and energetic — it is the last thing people will remember.

types.example: Good morning. I am Arjun Mehta. I hold a Master's degree in Business Administration from IIM Ahmedabad. I have 5 years of experience in financial consulting, specializing in risk management and investment strategy. I am passionate about creating data-driven financial solutions. My goal is to leverage my expertise to contribute meaningfully to your organization's growth. Thank you.
Hey! I'm Priya. I'm from Mumbai, currently doing my engineering in Pune. I love photography, hiking, and trying new food. I speak Hindi and English, and I'm learning French too. Nice to meet you all!

Hi, I'm Rahul Sharma, a full-stack developer with 4 years of experience building scalable web applications. I specialize in React and Node.js, and I've led teams of up to 8 developers. I'm passionate about creating user-friendly products that solve real-world problems. I'd love to connect and explore collaboration opportunities.
tips: End with a call to action

types.example: Good afternoon. My name is Deepika Verma. I am a third-year student pursuing a Bachelor's degree in English Literature from Miranda House, Delhi University. My research interest is in post-colonial literature, particularly the works of Chinua Achebe and Arundhati Roy. Outside academics, I organize debate competitions and lead the college literary club. I am excited to be here and look forward to productive discussions.

Hello everyone! Can you hear me clearly? Great. I am Vikram Joshi, joining from Hyderabad. I lead product development at ABC Tech, where we build enterprise SaaS solutions. I am looking forward to today's discussion. Please feel free to share your thoughts in the chat or unmute yourselves. Let's have a productive session.

Good morning to everyone. I am Neha Gupta from Jaipur, currently pursuing MBA at IIT Delhi, specializing in marketing. Before this, I worked for two years at a fintech startup handling customer acquisition. I bring strong analytical and communication skills to any discussion. I look forward to sharing ideas and building on everyone's perspectives today.
tips: Mention relevant skills for the discussion topic

grammarPoints.title: Key Grammar Points in Self Introduction
grammarPoints.hindiTitle: Self Introduction में जरूरी Grammar Points

points.title: Present Simple Tense — for facts and routines
points.hindi: Present Simple Tense — तथ्यों और नियमित कार्यों के लिए
points.explanation: Use Present Simple to state facts about yourself — your name, origin, job, hobbies.
examples: She teaches at a government school.

points.title: Present Continuous Tense — for ongoing activities
points.hindi: Present Continuous Tense — चल रहे कार्यों के लिए
points.explanation: Use Present Continuous to describe what you are doing right now or these days.
examples: I am currently working at TCS.
I am developing my leadership skills.

points.title: Past Simple Tense — for completed experiences
points.hindi: Past Simple Tense — पूरे हुए अनुभवों के लिए
points.explanation: Use Past Simple to describe your past education, jobs, or achievements.
examples: I graduated from Delhi University in 2021.
I worked at Infosys for two years.
I completed an internship at Google.

points.title: Articles — a, an, the
points.explanation: Correct use of articles is essential in self-introduction. Use "a" before consonant sounds, "an" before vowel sounds, and "the" for specific things.
points.formula: a + consonant sound word / an + vowel sound word / the + specific thing
examples: I work at the head office.

mistakes.correct: I work in the HR department.

points.title: Prepositions — in, at, from, for
points.explanation: Use the correct preposition with places, time, and purpose.

points.title: Indefinite Pronouns — I, My, Me, Myself
points.hindi: Indefinite Pronouns — I, My, Me, Myself
points.explanation: Use the correct personal pronoun depending on the grammatical role.

points.title: Have / Has — for experience
points.hindi: Have / Has — अनुभव बताने के लिए
points.explanation: Use "I have" for first person and "She/He has" for third person to describe experience.
points.formula: I/We/You/They + have + experience / He/She/It + has + experience
examples: He has led teams of 20 people.

speakingTips.title: 30 Expert Speaking Tips for Self Introduction

tips.tip: Always smile when you introduce yourself — it builds instant rapport.
Maintain eye contact throughout your introduction.
Speak at a moderate pace — not too fast, not too slow.
Use a confident, clear voice with good volume.
Practice your introduction in front of a mirror daily.
Record yourself and listen back to identify areas for improvement.
Start with a warm, genuine greeting.
Avoid using filler words like "umm", "uh", "you know".
Keep your introduction between 30 seconds and 2 minutes depending on context.
Organize your introduction in a logical sequence.
Use positive body language — stand tall, shoulders back.
Pause briefly between sentences for clarity and effect.
Emphasize key words like your name and achievements.
Use specific details rather than vague statements.
Practice with native English speakers or online platforms.
Vary your tone to sound engaging and natural.
Avoid memorizing word-for-word — understand the structure.
End with a strong, memorable closing statement.
Be authentic — genuine introductions are more impactful.
Adapt your introduction to the specific situation.
Use active voice rather than passive voice.
Mention one unique quality that makes you stand out.
Connect your introduction to the purpose of the meeting.
Use transitional phrases: "Moving on...", "In terms of..."
Be prepared for follow-up questions after your introduction.
Practice pronunciation of difficult words beforehand.
Use storytelling elements to make your introduction memorable.
Show genuine enthusiasm about your field or role.
Thank the audience for their time at the end.
Breathe! Deep breathing before speaking reduces anxiety.

tricks.explanation: N — Name, A — Activity (what you do), M — Mission (your goal), E — Enthusiasm (your passion)
tricks.example: N: My name is Rohan. A: I work as a developer. M: My mission is to build apps that help people. E: I am passionate about technology.

tricks.explanation: 30 seconds for name & origin, 30 seconds for profession & education, 30 seconds for goals & closing.
tricks.example: Use this for networking events where time is limited.

tricks.explanation: F — Family, O — Occupation, R — Recreation (hobbies), D — Dreams (goals)
tricks.example: Cover all four FORD areas for a complete introduction.

tricks.explanation: Stand in front of a mirror and practice your introduction every morning for 5 days. You will be amazed at the improvement.
tricks.example: Day 1: Focus on content. Day 2: Eye contact. Day 3: Tone. Day 4: Speed. Day 5: Full performance.

tricks.explanation: Start with an interesting fact, question, or quote instead of just "My name is..."
tricks.example: "Did you know that 93% of communication is non-verbal? Hi, I'm Rohan, and I study communication professionally..."
